sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the layout more clearly:
- Orange blocks at [[25, 29], [25, 33], [26, 33], [26, 29]] (cols 29-33)
- Blue blocks at rows 27-29, cols 29-33
- Obstacle (charcoal) at rows 30-32, cols 29-33
- White cross at rows 31-32, cols 20-22
The blocks moved LEFT successfully from cols 34-38 to cols 29-33. Now they're right above the obstacle.
Looking at the layout:
- Row 25-26: orange blocks at cols 29-33
- Row 27-29: blue blocks at cols 29-33
- Row 30-32: obstacle at cols 29-33
- White cross at rows 31-32, cols 20-22 (to the left of the obstacle)
The obstacle is directly below the blocks. The white cross is to the left of the obstacle. To reach the white cross area, I need to go around the obstacle.
Looking at the wider area:
- The gray area extends from cols 19-53 in the wider section
- The obstacle is at cols 29-33, rows 30-32 (L-shaped)
- To the left of the obstacle (cols 19-28) there's open gray space
- The white cross is at cols 20-22
So I need to move the blocks LEFT to get to cols 19-28, then DOWN to get past the obstacle, then maybe RIGHT to reach the white cross.
Wait, but the blocks are at cols 29-33, and the obstacle is also at cols 29-33. If I move LEFT, the blocks would go to cols 24-28, which is to the left of the obstacle. Then I could move DOWN.
Let me try moving LEFT again to get the blocks to the left of the obstacle.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Orange at [[25, 29], [25, 33], [26, 33], [26, 29]] (cols 29-33)
# Obstacle at cols 29-33, rows 30-32
# White cross at cols 20-22
# Need to move LEFT to get to cols 24-28 (left of obstacle)

result = action(['LEFT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"After LEFT - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
        break

# Check area around white cross
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nLeft area (rows 25-35, cols 18-30):")
for r in range(25, 36):
    print(f"  {r}: {lines[r][18:30]}")

# Try moving DOWN to get past obstacle level
result2 = action(['DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"\nAfter DOWN - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
